question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply crafted JVM arguments or environment variables at process startup via SpringApplication.run() to override hiero.mirror.importer.startDate to a future timestamp, causing the importer to silently skip all historical records and export nothing to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malformed hiero.mirror.importer.network value (e.g., 'mainnet-attacker') via an externalized config source so that getNetwork() strips the prefix and resolves to 'mainnet' while getNetworkPrefix() returns 'attacker', causing the importer to pull from an incorrect cloud bucket and export wrong records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.endDate to a timestamp in the past via an externalized Spring Boot config file, causing downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) to trigger immediately and all records after that date to be permanently missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.startBlockNumber to a very large value via an externalized config, causing setStreamFileIndex() to assign an incorrect index to the first stream file and break the sequential block ordering exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.endBlockNumber to 0 (satisfying @PositiveOrZero) via an externalized config, causing the importer to stop processing after the first block and export only a single record to the mirror node, making all subsequent records missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.consensusMode to EQUAL via an externalized config, causing ConsensusValidatorImpl to weight all nodes equally regardless of actual stake, so that a minority of colluding nodes can reach the 1/3 threshold and cause incorrect records to be accepted and exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.importHistoricalAccountInfo to false via an externalized config, causing historical account records to be permanently omitted from the mirror node export, resulting in missing records for all accounts created before the importer started?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.dataPath to a non-existent or attacker-controlled path via an externalized config, causing archiveFile() operations to silently fail and stream files to be lost before they are fully processed and exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.groupByDay to false via an externalized config, causing getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() to write all stream files into a flat directory, creating filename collisions that overwrite earlier records and cause incorrect data to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp to an incorrect value via an externalized config, causing topic running hash validation to use the wrong algorithm version and silently accept or reject records, leading to incorrect topic message records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition where SHA_384.isHashEmpty(expectedPreviousHash) returns true (when the previous hash is all zeros) to bypass the hash chain check entirely, allowing a stream file with a forged previousHash to be accepted and its incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where lastStreamFile holds an Optional.empty() (e.g., on first startup), causing expectedPreviousHash to be null and verifyHashChain() to return true unconditionally, allowing any stream file with any previousHash to be accepted and its records exported to the mirror node without chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a SignatureVerificationException to be thrown repeatedly by manipulating the cloud storage bucket contents, causing downloadNextBatch() to log a warning and silently skip entire batches of stream files, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEMO network check in downloadNextBatch() by setting the network to 'demo' via an externalized config, causing downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) to be triggered as soon as sigFilesMap is empty, permanently halting all future record downloads and leaving the mirror node with missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.downloadRatio to a very small value (e.g., 0.01) via an externalized config, causing partialCollection() to query signatures from only one node, making it trivial for that single node to supply incorrect stream files that pass consensus and export wrong records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Collections.shuffle() randomization in partialCollection() under a low downloadRatio setting, where the randomly selected subset of nodes happens to exclude all honest nodes, causing a malicious node's stream file to reach consensus and export incorrect records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.consensusRatio to 0 via an externalized config, causing ConsensusValidatorImpl.validate() to skip the consensus check entirely when signatureHashMap is non-empty, allowing a single node's signature to be sufficient for accepting and exporting any stream file content to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStakeRequiredForConsensus() throws SignatureVerificationException when totalStake is 0, causing the importer to crash and halt all record processing, resulting in all subsequent records being missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a node's public key to be null in the address book (e.g., by corrupting the address book file), causing verifySignature() to return false for that node and silently exclude its signatures from consensus, potentially allowing a minority of remaining nodes to reach the 1/3 threshold with incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifySignature() only verifies the metadataHashSignature when it is non-null, by supplying a signature file with a null metadataHashSignature, causing the metadata hash to be skipped during verification and allowing a stream file with tampered metadata to be accepted and its records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the continue statement in verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() when SignatureVerificationException is thrown for a non-last file, causing the importer to skip that file's records entirely and move to the next group, resulting in a gap of missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all data file downloads to fail with FileOperationException or HashMismatchException for every node that reached consensus, causing verifySignatures() to return false and log an error without retrying, resulting in that stream file's records being permanently missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endDate check in verifySignatures() where dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate) causes downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) and returns false, by manipulating the filename timestamp of a stream file to appear after endDate, causing all subsequent records to be permanently missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lastStreamFile AtomicReference update in onVerified() under a race condition between concurrent download threads, causing the lastStreamFile to be set to an out-of-order stream file, breaking the hash chain for subsequent files and causing records to be rejected or duplicated on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.startBlockNumber to -1 (satisfying @Min(-1)) via an externalized config, causing setStreamFileIndex() to assign index -1 to the first stream file, breaking block number ordering and causing incorrect block indices to be exported to the mirror node?
